label: Myanmar executes sixteen individuals linked to major cross-border telecom fraud syndicates
label_zh: 緬甸處決十六名與跨境電信詐騙集團有關人士

timeline.title: Judicial Reports Finalize Execution of Sixteen Fraud Ringleaders as Cross-Border Crackdown Intensifies
timeline.title_zh: 司法報告確認十六名詐騙頭目被處決，跨境打擊行動加劇
timeline.sub_title: China’s Supreme People's Court confirmed the execution of sixteen individuals linked to Myanmar-based syndicates, while recent junta announcements report dozens of additional deportations and arrests in the region.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 中國最高人民法院證實處決了十六名與緬甸詐騙集團有關聯的人員，同時緬甸軍政府近期宣布該地區有數十人被驅逐和逮捕。
timeline.summary:
In a definitive conclusion to one of the largest transnational law enforcement operations in recent years, China’s top judicial authorities have finalized reports confirming the execution of sixteen ringleaders associated with major telecom fraud syndicates operating out of northern Myanmar. The confirmation was presented during the annual work reports of the Supreme People's Court (SPC) and the Supreme People's Procuratorate (SPP) at the 14th National People's Congress in Beijing, which concluded its current session on March 12, 2026. The reports identified these individuals as core members of notorious family-led criminal organizations that had long operated with impunity in the lawless borderlands of Myanmar’s Kokang region.

According to the judicial findings, the executed individuals belonged primarily to the Ming and Bai family syndicates, which transformed the Kokang area into a global hub for "pig-butchering" scams and illegal online gambling. The Ming family syndicate, led by figures such as Ming Guoping and Ming Zhenzhen, was found responsible for establishing fortified compounds in Laukkai where thousands of trafficked laborers were forced to conduct digital fraud. These operations were characterized by extreme violence, including intentional homicide, illegal detention, and torture. One of the most severe incidents cited by the court involved gang members opening fire on captive Chinese nationals in October 2023 to prevent their repatriation, resulting in multiple deaths.

The scale of the criminal enterprise was staggering, with the SPC confirming that the syndicates had swindled more than 10 billion yuan (approximately $1.4 billion) from victims across the globe since 2015. The crackdown, which intensified throughout 2024 and 2025, resulted in the repatriation of over 53,000 suspects from Myanmar to China. Of the sixteen ringleaders executed, eleven members of the Ming family group were put to death in early 2026 following a first-instance verdict in Wenzhou, while four leaders of the Bai family syndicate were executed in February after their cases were processed in Shenzhen. One additional death sentence was carried out following the top court's final review of the groups' combined crimes, which included drug trafficking and the production of nearly eleven tonnes of methamphetamine.

Developments on March 12, 2026, indicate that while the most prominent leaders have been neutralized, the enforcement campaign remains active. The Myanmar military junta announced the deportation of 73 more foreign nationals suspected of involvement in telecom fraud, alongside the arrest of eight additional suspects. Regional reports [ref:3] also confirmed the arrest of a Chinese citizen in Mon State who had allegedly fled Thai law enforcement to continue fraud activities within Myanmar. These ongoing actions coincide with the formal dissolution of the Fully Light Group, a Kokang-based conglomerate that had served as a primary financial vehicle for the syndicates.

As the NPC session closes, China’s Chief Justice Zhang Jun and Prosecutor-general Ying Yong have pledged a "zero-tolerance" approach for the remainder of 2026. Future efforts will focus on preventing the relocation of scam hubs to southern Shan State and deepening cooperation with Thailand and Laos to eliminate the remaining infrastructure of these transnational criminal networks.
timeline.summary_zh:
在近年來最大規模的跨國執法行動之一畫下決定性句點之際，中國最高司法機關已完成報告，確認處決了十六名與活躍在緬甸北部的主要電信詐騙集團有關聯的頭目。此確認是在2026年3月12日閉幕的第十四屆全國人民代表大會上，由最高人民法院（SPC）和最高人民檢察院（SPP）的年度工作報告中提出的。報告指認這些人是臭名昭著的家族式犯罪組織的核心成員，這些組織長期在緬甸果敢地區無法無天的邊境地帶逍遙法外。

根據司法調查結果，被處決的個人主要屬於明氏和白氏家族集團，他們將果敢地區變成了全球「殺豬盤」騙局和非法網絡賭博的中心。以明國平、明珍珍等人為首的明氏家族集團，被發現在老街建立了設防的營地，數千名被販運的勞工被迫在那裡進行數字詐騙。這些行動以極端的暴力為特徵，包括故意殺人、非法拘禁和酷刑。法院引述的最嚴重事件之一，涉及該幫派成員於2023年10月向被俘的中國公民開槍，以阻止他們被遣返，造成多人死亡。

犯罪企業的規模驚人，SPC確認自2015年以來，這些集團已從全球受害者手中詐騙了超過100億人民幣（約合14億美元）。此次打擊行動在2024年至2025年間不斷加強，導致超過53,000名嫌疑人從緬甸被遣返回中國。在被處決的十六名頭目中，十一名前明氏家族集團成員於2026年初在溫州一審判決後被處決，而四名前白氏家族集團領導人在案件於深圳處理後於2月被處決。在最高法院對該集團的共同罪行（包括販毒和生產近十一噸甲基苯丙胺）進行最終審查後，執行了一項額外的死刑判決。

2026年3月12日的發展表明，儘管最突出的領導人已被清除，但執法行動仍在繼續。緬甸軍政府宣布驅逐了73名涉嫌參與電信詐騙的外國國民，並逮捕了另外八名嫌疑人。地區報告也證實，一名中國公民在孟邦被捕，據稱他逃避泰國執法部門的追捕，繼續在緬甸進行詐騙活動。這些持續的行動恰逢果敢地區的「全明集團」（Fully Light Group）正式解散，該集團曾是詐騙集團的主要金融工具。[ref:3]

隨著全國人大會議閉幕，中國首席大法官張軍和檢察長應勇承諾在2026年剩餘時間內採取「零容忍」態度。未來的努力將重點放在防止詐騙中心轉移到撣邦南部，並深化與泰國和老撾的合作，以消除這些跨國犯罪網絡的殘餘基礎設施。

timeline.title: China’s Highest Courts Formally Confirm Execution of Sixteen Ringleaders from Myanmar Fraud Syndicates
timeline.title_zh: 中國最高法院正式確認處決16名緬甸詐騙集團首腦
timeline.sub_title: Judicial reports delivered at the National People’s Congress finalize the sentences for core members of the ‘Four Families’ following a multi-year crackdown on transnational cybercrime.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 在全國人民代表大會上發布的司法報告，在對跨國網絡犯罪進行多年打擊後，最終確定了「四大家族」核心成員的判決。
timeline.summary:
In a definitive closure to one of the most significant transnational law enforcement efforts in Southeast Asian history, China’s top judicial authorities have officially confirmed the execution of 16 high-ranking members of northern Myanmar’s notorious telecom fraud syndicates. The announcement was made during the annual plenary meetings of the National People’s Congress in Beijing, where Zhang Jun, President of the Supreme People’s Court, and Ying Yong, the Procurator-General, delivered their 2025 work reports. The documents underscore a ‘zero tolerance’ approach toward criminal networks that have exploited Chinese citizens through digital scams, human trafficking, and violent coercion in the lawless border regions of Myanmar’s Kokang territory.

The executed individuals were primarily associated with the so-called ‘Four Major Families’—influential clans that previously dominated the political and criminal landscape of northern Myanmar. These groups, including the Ming and Bai families, allegedly leveraged armed factions to protect massive scam compounds where thousands of victims were held against their will. Judicial records reveal that the syndicates’ activities resulted in the deaths of at least 14 Chinese nationals and injuries to many others. Financial assessments indicate that [ref:1, 7, 9, 4, 5, 8] the criminal proceeds from these operations, which included illicit gambling and drug trafficking alongside telecom fraud, surpassed 10 billion yuan ($1.4 billion) over a decade.

The 16 executions were carried out across several jurisdictions, with major group executions reported in Wenzhou, Zhejiang province, and Shenzhen, Guangdong province. Among those identified in the final reports were core leaders such as Mg Myin Shaunt Phyin and Ma Thiri Maung. In one high-profile case involving [ref:5] five members of the Bay (Bai) syndicate, four were executed in early February after the Supreme People's Court approved their sentences, while the fifth member died of illness while in custody. The reports highlighted that the legal [ref:3] process followed strict standards, with the top court reviewing all evidence to ensure convictions for intentional homicide and fraud were ‘conclusive and sufficient.’

This judicial milestone follows an unprecedented wave of regional cooperation that began in late 2023. Under intense pressure from Beijing, Myanmar’s military government and various ethnic armed groups facilitated the repatriation of over 40,000 fraud suspects to China throughout 2025. This collaborative effort effectively dismantled the ‘protective umbrellas’ previously enjoyed by these syndicates. Beyond the executions, the Supreme People’s Procuratorate reported that 285 key suspects were prosecuted for serious violent crimes related to these operations, with an additional 16 receiving life sentences and seven others sentenced to death with a two-year reprieve.

Looking forward, Chinese authorities have pledged to further intensify their crackdown on cross-border cybercrime in 2026. The judicial reports emphasize that the success of the Myanmar operations serves as a blueprint for targeting similar syndicates in Cambodia, Thailand, and Laos. By prioritizing the prosecution of high-level financiers and ‘armed protectors,’ Beijing aims to permanently disrupt the structural conditions that allowed the multi-billion-dollar scam industry to flourish. International observers note that while these executions send a powerful deterrent message, the shifting of criminal networks to other Southeast Asian jurisdictions remains a persistent challenge for regional security.
timeline.summary_zh:
在東南亞歷史上最重大的跨國執法行動之一的明確閉幕中，中國最高司法當局已正式確認處決了緬甸北部惡名昭彰的電信詐騙集團的16名高級成員。此 announcement 是在北京舉行的全國人民代表大會年度全體會議期間發布的，最高人民法院院長張軍和最高人民檢察院檢察長應勇發表了他們2025年的工作報告。這些文件強調了對透過數位詐騙、人口販運和暴力脅迫剝削中國公民的犯罪網絡的「零容忍」態度，這些活動發生在緬甸撣邦果敢地區的無法無天邊境地區。

被處決的個人主要與所謂的「四大家族」有關——這些是先前主宰緬甸北部政治和犯罪格局的有影響力的氏族。這些集團，包括明氏和白氏家族，據稱利用武裝派系保護大規模詐騙營地，數千名受害者被非法拘禁。司法記錄顯示，該集團的活動至少造成14名中國公民死亡，多人受傷。財務評估表明，這些行動的犯罪所得，包括非法賭博和毒品販運以及電信詐騙，十年來超過100億元人民幣（14億美元）。[ref:1, 7, 9, 4, 5, 8]

16次處決發生在多個司法管轄區，其中浙江溫州和廣東深圳報告了大規模集團處決。在最終報告中確認的核心領導人包括Mg Myin Shaunt Phyin和Ma Thiri Maung。在一宗涉及白氏集團五名成員的備受矚目案件中，在最高人民法院批准其判決後，四人在二月初被處決，而第五名成員在羈押期間死於疾病。報告強調，法律程序遵循嚴格標準，最高法院審查了所有證據，以確保故意殺人罪和詐騙罪的定罪「確鑿充分」。[ref:5, 3]

這一司法里程碑是繼2023年底開始的史無前例的區域合作浪潮之後的。在北京的強烈壓力下，緬甸軍政府和各民族武裝團體協助將超過40,000名詐騙嫌疑人在2025年期間遣返中國。這一合作努力有效地瓦解了這些集團先前享有的「保護傘」。除了處決外，最高人民檢察院報告稱，有285名主要嫌疑人因與這些行動有關的嚴重暴力犯罪被起訴，另有16人被判處無期徒刑，七人被判處死刑緩期兩年執行。

展望未來，中國當局已承諾在2026年進一步加強對跨國網絡犯罪的打擊。司法報告強調，緬甸行動的成功為針對柬埔寨、泰國和老撾的類似集團提供了藍圖。透過優先起訴高級金融家和「武裝保護者」，北京旨在永久破壞允許數十億美元詐騙產業蓬勃發展的結構性條件。國際觀察家指出，雖然這些處決發出了強烈的威懾信號，但犯罪網絡轉移到東南亞其他地區仍然是區域安全的一個持續挑戰。

timeline.title: China Confirms Execution of Sixteen Ringleaders Linked to Major Myanmar-Based Telecom Fraud Syndicates
timeline.title_zh: 中國證實處決十六名與緬甸電信詐騙集團首腦有關人員
timeline.sub_title: Official judicial reports released during Beijing's annual parliamentary meetings confirm the execution of key figures from the notorious 'Four Major Families' operating across the Myanmar-China border.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 北京年度議會期間發布的官方司法報告證實，已處決了活躍於緬甸-中國邊境的著名「四大族」的關鍵人物。
timeline.summary:
In a major update to the ongoing crackdown on transnational crime, China’s Supreme People’s Court (SPC) and Supreme People’s Procuratorate (SPP) released annual work reports on March 9 and 10, 2026, confirming the execution of sixteen ringleaders from Myanmar-based telecommunications fraud syndicates. The reports, delivered during the National People's Congress in Beijing, mark the formal conclusion of high-profile cases against the 'Four Major Families' of the Kokang region. These families—most notably the Ming and Bai clans—had for years operated industrial-scale scam compounds protected by private armed militias.

According to the judicial findings, the sixteen executed individuals were the principal architects of a criminal enterprise that swindled victims out of more than 10 billion yuan (approximately $1.4 billion). Beyond financial fraud, the syndicates were convicted of violent crimes including intentional homicide, kidnapping, and illegal detention. The SPC confirmed [ref:1] that the brutal conditions within the 'scam farms' led to the deaths of at least 14 Chinese citizens and the injury of dozens more who attempted to escape or failed to meet scamming quotas. The dismantling of these networks involved the repatriation of over 41,000 suspects from northern Myanmar to China throughout 2025.

The reports detailed a stratified sentencing structure for the 285 core members of the syndicates prosecuted in Chinese courts. While sixteen received immediate execution, another sixteen were handed life sentences, and seven were granted death sentences with a two-year reprieve. The SPP highlighted that these trials serve as a 'decisive blow' to cross-border crime, underscoring a strategic reset in regional law enforcement. This operation was characterized by rare levels of cooperation between Chinese authorities and the Myanmar junta, which had faced immense pressure to hand over the powerful crime bosses previously operating with local impunity.

International human rights organizations and the United Nations have characterized the situation in these border zones as a 'wicked problem,' noting that hundreds of thousands of people have been trafficked into forced labor within the compounds. While the execution of the ringleaders addresses the leadership of the syndicates, experts warn that the underlying infrastructure of the scam industry remains resilient. In response, Chinese prosecutors pledged in their March 10 update to continue intensifying their crackdown through 2026, focusing on the 'online ecosystem' and further dismantling the financial backers and communication networks that sustain these transnational operations.
timeline.summary_zh:
在持續打擊跨國犯罪的行動中，中國最高人民法院 (SPC) 和最高人民檢察院 (SPP) 於 2026 年 3 月 9 日和 10 日發布了年度工作報告，證實已處決了來自緬甸電信詐騙集團的十六名首腦。在這次北京全國人民代表大會上發表的報告標誌著針對 Kokang 地區「四大族」的重大案件正式結案。這些家族——尤其是 Ming 和 Bai 氏族——多年來一直經營著由私人武裝民兵保護的工業規模詐騙窩點。
根據司法調查結果，這十六名被處決的個人是一個犯罪企業的主要策劃者，該企業詐騙受害者超過 100 億元人民幣（約合 14 億美元）。除了金融詐騙，這些犯罪集團還被判犯有暴力罪行，包括故意殺人、綁架和非法拘禁。最高人民法院證實，這些「詐騙農場」內的殘酷條件導致至少 14 名中國公民死亡，另有數十人在試圖逃跑或未能達到詐騙配額後受傷。在 2025 年全年，有超過 41,000 名嫌疑人從緬甸北部被遣返中國，從而瓦解了這些犯罪網絡。[ref:1]
報告詳細說明了中國法院起訴的 285 名集團核心成員的等級化判決結構。其中十六人被立即處決，另有十六人被判處無期徒刑，七人被判處死刑緩期執行兩年。最高人民檢察院強調，這些審判是對跨境犯罪的「決定性打擊」，並強調了區域執法能力的戰略重置。此次行動的特點是中國當局與緬甸軍政府之間罕見的高度合作，此前軍政府一直面臨巨大壓力，要求其交出先前在當地享有豁免權的強大犯罪頭目。
國際人權組織和聯合國將這些邊境地區的情況描述為一個「棘手的問題」，指出有數十萬人被販運到這些窩點進行強迫勞動。儘管處決了這些首腦解決了犯罪集團的領導層問題，但專家警告說，詐騙行業的基礎設施依然具有韌性。為此，中國檢察官在 3 月 10 日的更新中承諾，將在 2026 年繼續加強打擊力度，重點關注「網絡生態系統」，並進一步瓦解維持這些跨國運營的金融支持者和通信網絡。

timeline.title: China Confirms Execution of Sixteen Telecom Fraud Ringleaders Following Dismantling of Myanmar Crime Syndicates
timeline.title_zh: 中國最高人民法院發布里程碑式司法報告後，處決16名緬甸電信詐騙集團首腦
timeline.sub_title: Annual judicial reports released during Beijing's Two Sessions confirm the execution of key leaders from the notorious 'Four Major Families' who operated multibillion-dollar scam networks in northern Myanmar.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 在全國人民代表大會期間，中國最高人民法院確認處決了來自緬甸北部惡名昭彰的犯罪家族的16名主要成員，這些家族涉及數十億美元的詐騙活動。
timeline.summary:
In a decisive conclusion to one of the largest transnational law enforcement operations in recent years, Chinese authorities confirmed on Monday the immediate execution of sixteen principal members of major cross-border telecom fraud syndicates. The announcement was made through annual work reports released by the Supreme People’s Procuratorate and the Supreme People’s Court during the ongoing 'Two Sessions' parliamentary meetings in Beijing. The reports highlight the complete dismantling of the 'Four Major Families' of the Kokang region in northern Myanmar, criminal organizations that for years utilized armed protection to run industrial-scale scam compounds and gambling dens.

The executed individuals were primarily high-ranking members of the Ming and Bai family syndicates. The judicial process, which culminated in these executions, revealed a harrowing pattern of violence used to maintain control over scam operations. Evidence [ref:5, 1, 6, 3] presented during trials in cities like Wenzhou and Shenzhen confirmed that these groups were responsible for not only defrauding thousands of victims of over 39 billion yuan (approximately $5.4 billion) but also committing heinous violent crimes. These included intentional homicide, illegal detention, and torture. One of the most notorious incidents cited involved gang members opening fire on captive Chinese nationals in October 2023 to prevent their repatriation, resulting in multiple fatalities.

Among the sixteen executed were key figures such as Ming Guoping and Ming Zhenzhen, who led the Ming family’s operations in the Laukkai and Shiyuanzi areas. Their syndicate alone was responsible for the deaths of at least fourteen Chinese citizens. The Bai family [ref:5, 1], another pillar of the Kokang criminal underworld, saw four of its leading members executed following convictions for telecom fraud and drug trafficking. Their leader [ref:7, 4], Bai Suocheng, reportedly died of illness shortly after his conviction, escaping the execution carried out against his subordinates. The reports state that a total of 39 individuals from these groups received life imprisonment or death sentences, marking a significant victory for regional security.

The scale of the crackdown reflects a massive collaborative effort between Chinese and Myanmar law enforcement. Over the past year, more than 57,000 Chinese nationals suspected of involvement in these scam operations have been arrested and repatriated. Chinese courts have concluded over 27,000 cases related to northern Myanmar fraud hubs, sentencing more than 41,000 suspects. The 'Four Major [ref:2] Families' had long operated with a sense of impunity, often holding positions in local government or militias to provide a 'protective umbrella' for their illicit activities, making their eventual downfall a landmark event in the fight against human trafficking and cybercrime.

International observers have noted that these executions serve as a stark warning to other criminal enterprises operating in Southeast Asia's lawless border zones. The United Nations had previously estimated that over 120,000 people were trapped in forced labor situations within Myanmar’s scam compounds. With the core leadership [ref:1] of the Kokang syndicates now eliminated, Chinese authorities have pledged to further intensify their crackdown in 2026, targeting the remaining remnants of these networks and their financial backers to ensure the safety and property of citizens across the region.
timeline.summary_zh:
在國際執法和區域安全方面邁出了重要一步，中國最高人民法院（SPC）於2026年3月9日宣布，16名位於緬甸北部的主要電信詐騙集團成員已被處決。這一消息是提交給北京第14屆全國人民代表大會第二次會議的法院工作報告的重點。由最高人民法院院長張軍作出的報告，強調了政府瓦解「四大家族」的決心——這些權勢滔天的類似黑手黨的氏族多年來控制著緬甸撣邦果敢地區的無法無天邊境地區。

被處決的個人被確認為犯罪網絡的高級領導層，這些網絡將老撾鎮變成了線上詐騙、人口販賣和暴力犯罪的全球中心。據最高人民法院稱，這些組織負責策劃大規模的「殺豬盤」騙局和加密貨幣詐騙，從受害者手中騙取了數百億人民幣。除了金融犯罪 [ref:1] 外，這些犯罪集團還被判犯有故意殺人、綁架以及非法拘禁等駭人聽聞的罪行，這些被拘禁的工人經常被販賣到詐騙營地，並在遭受酷刑的威脅下被迫工作。

司法報告詳細說明，兩個最臭名昭著的犯罪集團，由明氏和白氏家族領導，已被「徹底瓦解」。2026年初的先前法院記錄顯示，1月份處決了11名明氏家族成員，2月初處決了4名白氏家族成員。3月9日報告的16名處決總數，標誌著這些案件的最終結果，也是首次有如此高級別的「詐騙頭目」在被遣返回中國後面臨最終的懲罰。報告還指出，一名著名的首腦白所成因病在預定處決前死亡，而另一名明學昌則於2023年底自殺以逃避抓捕。

這次司法打擊是在中國當局與緬甸政府機構前所未有的合作時期之後進行的。過去一年，加強的外交和軍事壓力導致數萬名嫌疑人被遣返，其中包括近期報告中明確點名的285名關鍵人物。雖然有16人被判處死刑並立即執行，但最高人民法院確認，另有39名被告被判處終身監禁，數百人因在跨境犯罪企業中的角色被判處不同刑期。

處決行動對在東南亞活動的其他犯罪組織發出了嚴厲警告。中國官員強調，在海外犯下的侵犯中國公民權利和利益的犯罪將受到法律的嚴懲。隨著全國人民代表大會 [ref:2] 的繼續進行，報告的調查結果被譽為打擊網絡犯罪的一大勝利，標誌著中國司法跨境觸角的積極新時代。
